gauge.name: Meramec River at Fenton

impacts.statement: The house at the corner of Main and Ferry begins flooding near this height.
The Sisters Teahouse on Main Street begins flooding near this height.
Denny Dennis Sporting Goods begins flooding at this height.
Water reaches the front door of the American Legion Hall.
The levee protecting the MSD sewage plant is overtopped.
The lower storage level of the American Legion Hall begins flooding.
Opps Lane residence downstream of gage begins flooding.
Opps Lane and Tosti's Transmission on Gravois Road begin flooding at this height.
An auto glass shop on Gravois begins flooding near this height.
Joe Clark's Restaurant begins flooding at this height.
White building on Gravois across from Joe Clark's begins flooding at this height.
Brannan's Marine and Motorsports begin flooding at this height.
Old 141 begins flooding east of the Gravois intersection.
Heating and cooling store begins flooding at this height.
Gravois Road begins flooding in Fenton southwest of the new bridge.
The Fenton Feed Mill begins flooding near this height.
Water begins backing onto Water Street at the riverside drain at the Ferry Street intersection.
Restrooms and playground at Minnie Ha Ha Park and the gazebo at Fenton Park begin flooding near this height.
Minnie Ha Ha Park gazebo begins flooding.
At this height, the entire boat ramp path at Minnie Ha Ha Park will be submerged.
The pedestrian walkway along the Meramec Greenway begins flooding near this height about halfway between the Highway 30 bridge and the Greenway gazebo.
Pedestrain trail along Opps Lane begins flooding near the MSD treatment plant.
Near this height, the top of the boat ramp at Minnie Ha Ha Park will be submerged.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
